title: pyrus seguinii
intro: Pyrus seguinii, also known as the Northwest crabapple, is a deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as sore throats and skin irritations. The health benefits of Pyrus seguinii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Pyrus seguinii typically involve infusing the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
